birth.definition: the event of a baby being born
birth.example: She made a birth plan for how she wanted her baby to arrive.

relief.definition: something that reduces pain or makes you feel better
relief.example: She wanted a birth with all the pain relief available.

float.definition: to rest on top of water without sinking
float.example: Some women want to float in a pool during birth.

pool.definition: a small body of water; here a special tub used during birth
pool.example: A birthing pool can help you feel relaxed.

desirable.definition: something you want, something that seems good
desirable.example: Plan whatever seems most desirable to you.

traumatic.definition: very shocking and upsetting, causing lasting emotional pain
traumatic.example: Plan the least traumatic birth to give you a good start.

epidural.definition: medicine given through the back to stop pain during birth
epidural.example: A planned epidural may turn out not to be possible.

emergency.definition: a sudden dangerous situation that needs quick action
emergency.example: A natural birth may end up as an emergency Caesarean.

caesarean.definition: a medical operation to take a baby out through the mother's stomach
caesarean.example: Her natural birth ended up as an emergency Caesarean.

flexible.definition: able to change and accept different things easily
flexible.example: Stay flexible about possible changes to your birth plan.

peaceful.definition: quiet, calm, and free from worry
peaceful.example: She wanted a calm, natural, peaceful birth.

cord.definition: the tube that connects a baby to its mother inside the body
cord.example: The cord was wrapped three times around the baby's neck.

ventouse.definition: a suction cup device used to help pull the baby out during birth
ventouse.example: The baby had to be pulled out by ventouse extraction.

extraction.definition: the act of pulling something out
extraction.example: The baby was delivered by ventouse extraction.

whisked.example: The baby had to be whisked off to a special-care unit.

special-care.definition: a hospital area for babies who need extra medical attention
special-care.example: The baby was taken to a special-care unit for observation.

skin-on-skin.definition: holding a newborn baby directly against your bare skin
skin-on-skin.example: She wanted skin-on-skin contact right after birth.

precautions.definition: careful actions taken to prevent something bad from happening
precautions.example: Precautions were taken because there might have been a problem.

unit.definition: a section or department in a hospital
unit.example: She found the special-care unit and stayed with her baby.

staff.definition: the people who work at a place like a hospital
staff.example: Try as they might, the staff could not make her leave.

dipped.definition: went down suddenly to a lower level
dipped.example: The baby's heartbeat dipped, so they had to act fast.

heartbeat.definition: the regular sound your heart makes as it pumps blood
heartbeat.example: The baby's heartbeat dipped during the birth.

wrapped.definition: wound around something in a circle
wrapped.example: The cord was wrapped three times around the baby's neck.

steer.definition: to guide or direct something in a particular direction
steer.example: All you can do is steer in the direction you want to go.

research.definition: careful study to find out new information about something
research.example: It is worth taking time to do some research about birth options.

accordingly.definition: in a way that matches or follows what was planned
accordingly.example: She made a birth plan accordingly.

loss.definition: the feeling of sadness from not having something you wanted
loss.example: She felt a sense of loss when they took the baby away.

emotional.definition: having strong feelings, or causing strong feelings
emotional.example: It took a while before she could tell the story without becoming emotional.
